brief_summary: Three-panel cross-section diagram showing how acoustic energy from switch bottom-out travels through each case material. Aluminium: thick walls absorb and trap high-frequency energy (dashed arrows, short escape paths). Polycarbonate: thinner, less-dense walls allow more energy to escape the cavity (solid escape arrows in all directions, including upward), producing a brighter character. ABS: moderate escape with a hollow-cavity annotation indicating the ring that foam mods address. Ochre arrows trace the energy flow; dashed lines indicate absorption.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/case-materials-compared.mdx § 'Aluminium' — 'Dense walls damp high frequencies; the case becomes a near-silent resonant chamber'
apps/web/src/content/articles/case-materials-compared.mdx § 'Polycarbonate' — 'Less dense than aluminium so more acoustic energy escapes rather than being absorbed. The resonance is brighter'
apps/web/src/content/articles/case-materials-compared.mdx § 'ABS' — 'Slight hollowness without foam modifications, but easily dampened'
